patterns.name: SELECT_STAR
patterns.message: SELECT * fetches all columns including large JSON fields. Select only needed columns to reduce I/O.
patterns.fix: Replace SELECT * with explicit column list: SELECT col1, col2, col3

patterns.name: NO_WHERE_CLAUSE
patterns.message: No WHERE clause — query will perform a full table scan reading every row.
patterns.fix: Add WHERE clause with an indexed column to filter rows before scanning

patterns.name: JSON_EXTRACT_IN_WHERE
patterns.message: JSON_EXTRACT in WHERE clause cannot use standard B-tree indexes, causing full table scan.
patterns.fix: Add a generated column with the extracted value and index it

patterns.name: NESTED_SUBQUERY
patterns.message: Nested subquery in WHERE — may execute as correlated subquery evaluating once per outer row.
patterns.fix: Rewrite as a JOIN or use a CTE (WITH clause) for better optimization

patterns.name: NO_LIMIT
patterns.message: No LIMIT clause — query may return unbounded result set consuming excessive memory and network.

patterns.name: CARTESIAN_JOIN
patterns.message: JOIN without ON clause produces a cartesian product multiplying row counts.
patterns.fix: Add ON clause specifying the join condition between tables

patterns.name: UPDATE_WITHOUT_WHERE
patterns.message: UPDATE without WHERE clause will modify ALL rows in the table!
patterns.fix: Add WHERE clause to scope the UPDATE to intended rows only
